title: Astracantha octopus
intro: Astracantha octopus, also known as Astragalus octopus, is a perennial legume plant belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, cardiovascular problems, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Astracantha octopus include alkaloids, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of capsules or tablets, to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Astracantha octopus is a species of spiny shrub in the pea family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Astracantha. The genus Astracantha is a group of thorny shrubs native to the Mediterranean and Middle Eastern regions. The classification of Astracantha octopus places it within the flowering plant kingdom, specifically within the class Magnoliopsida. Its classification is further detailed at the family and subfamily levels.
